privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a procedurally stripped version of the official SweetHome3DExample7.sh3d contemporary villa sample file to generate the 'botanical_starter.sh3d' shell. By independently parsing the generated baseline file, I verified it contains exactly 48 walls, 19 defined rooms (8 of which already have floor colors/textures), and 0 furniture items. The passing threshold is strictly enforced at 70 out of 100 possible points. However, I discovered a critical defect in the scoring logic: while the task explicitly instructs the agent to create 4 specific named rooms ('Ticketing', 'Gift Shop', etc.) and apply floor colors to 3, `verifier.py` completely ignores the exported `room_names` array and incorrectly evaluates the absolute `room_count` and `rooms_with_floor_color` rather than using the calculated `new_rooms` delta. Because the pre-existing baseline shell already significantly exceeds these thresholds (19 rooms, 8 colored), Criterion 5 (15 points) is perpetually satisfied by default as soon as the agent places enough items to pass the minimum 15-item furniture gate.
